items.title: Forget the 2.8% Social Security Increase. These Aristocrats Pay You 4% to 7% More Annually
items.description: The Social Security Administration announced in October that beneficiaries will receive a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) in 2026, following a 2.5% increase in 2025. While these adjustments help protect purchasing power for 71 million Americans, dividend growth stocks have historically delivered substantially higher annual increases. items.title: Looking for a Consumer Staples ETF? Here's How XLP and RSPS Compare on Cost, Risk, and Earnings
items.author: Katie Brockman
items.description: Two consumer staples ETFs, XLP and RSPS, offer different investment approaches: XLP focuses on market-cap-weighted large companies with lower fees, while RSPS provides equal-weighted exposure across the sector with broader diversification.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Lower expense ratio (0.08%), larger assets under management ($15.5 billion), and focused strategy on sector leaders with potential for higher returns
Top holding in XLP, representing a significant portion of the ETF's assets
Top holding in XLP, contributing to the fund's strong market position
